title: Kitten Teething Guide: What to Do if You Find a Tooth
meta_desc: Finding a kitten's baby tooth on the floor is usually normal. Learn about the teething process and when to contact a vet in our triage guide.
content_html:
What exactly did you find?

Finding a small, white, crown-shaped object on your floor can be a surprise, but if your kitten is between 3 and 7 months old, you have likely just found a deciduous tooth, commonly known as a baby tooth. Kittens have 26 baby teeth that are eventually replaced by 30 adult teeth. While kittens often swallow these teeth during meals, finding one occasionally is a perfectly normal part of their development.

Is finding a tooth an emergency?

The urgency level for this situation is Low. Teething is a natural biological process and not a medical emergency. You may notice your kitten chewing more than usual, having slightly red gums, or even leaving a tiny spot of blood on a chew toy. These are all standard symptoms. However, if your kitten is unable to eat, seems lethargic, or has significant bleeding, you should contact your veterinarian for a routine check-up.

Why a photo helps with triage

If you are worried that the tooth looks broken or if the gums look excessively swollen, taking a clear, well-lit photo of both the tooth and your kitten's mouth is very helpful. A photo allows a veterinary professional to quickly distinguish between a naturally shed baby tooth and a painful fracture of an adult tooth. It also helps us monitor the gum line for 'retained' baby teeth, which occur when a baby tooth refuses to fall out as the adult tooth emerges.

faqs.question: At what age do kittens lose their baby teeth?
faqs.answer: Most kittens begin losing their baby teeth around 12 to 14 weeks of age, and the process is usually complete by the time they are 6 or 7 months old.

faqs.question: Should I be worried about blood on my kitten's toys?
faqs.answer: A small amount of spotting is normal when a tooth is lost. However, if the bleeding is persistent or heavy, you should consult your veterinarian.

faqs.question: What should I do if my kitten has two teeth in the same spot?
faqs.answer: This is called a persistent deciduous tooth. If it doesn't fall out on its own within a week, it should be evaluated by a vet to prevent crowding or bite issues.
